title: Tunisian Rest-Meat Brik
description: A comforting and flavorful Tunisian dish made with rest-meat, turmeric, and low-sodium spices. Serve with a side of fresh vegetables for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the rest-meat and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Stir in the turmeric, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 1 minute.
Beat the egg in a separate bowl and set aside.
In a separate pan, heat a small amount of oil over medium heat.
Sprinkle a small amount of flour into the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Gradually add the water to the flour mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked rest-meat mixture to the egg and stir to combine.
Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and stir until smooth.
Season the mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Using a pastry brush, brush the edges of a brik pastry with water.
Place a tablespoon of the rest-meat mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the pastry with water and fold the other half over the filling.
Press the edges of the pastry together to seal the brik.
Repeat with the remaining pastry and filling.
Place the briks on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with olive oil.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Serve the briks hot with a side of fresh vegetables.
